methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vertical Aerospace is a public-stage transportation company that has raised over $400M to date. The valuation universe for electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) and advanced transportation comprises five private peers—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables currently available in the narrow peer set.

Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable to this analysis. The transportation sector contains only one same-stage comparable, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches, falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for robust peer analysis. An all-sector median approach would introduce substantial distortion given the heterogeneity of transportation subsectors and development stages represented in the broader comp universe.

Without a sufficient narrow peer set, traditional triangulation cannot be reliably executed. A defensible valuation would require alternative methods: precedent transaction analysis within eVTOL and advanced mobility, discounted cash flow modeling anchored to addressable market sizing and production ramp assumptions, or sum-of-the-parts analysis if Vertical Aerospace has discrete business units. The primary uncertainty remains the absence of public market pricing signals for eVTOL operators, limiting ability to benchmark expected exit multiples. Any valuation estimate at this stage should be explicitly caveated as preliminary pending either expansion of comparable data or completion of alternative methodologies.
